article_title: Deep-Sea Mining Regulations Stall at ISA as Scientists Catalog 788 Unknown Species in the Zone Slated for Extraction

content_preview.title: Deep-Sea Mining Regulations Stall at ISA as Scientists Catalog 788 Unknown Species in the Zone Slated for Extraction
content_preview.summary: The ISA adjourned without completing deep-sea mining rules as a Nature study found 90 percent of species in the target zone remain unknown and the US fast-tracks its own permits.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The International Seabed Authority adjourned its 31st annual session on March 19, 2026, without establishing a timeline for completing the mining code that would govern commercial extraction of polymetallic nodules from the deep ocean floor. The regulatory impasse arrives at a moment of acute tension: a five-year study published in [Nature Ecology and Evolution](https://nature.com/articles/s41559-025-02911-4) in February cataloged 788 species from the Clarion-Clipperton Zone between...

recommendations: Strong analysis piece covering multiple dimensions of the deep-sea mining debate
NPR and Scientific American sources independently confirm key claims including species counts and NOAA rule changes
Nature source returned 303 redirect but the study details are corroborated by secondary coverage
